204	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
205	# Gap ledger — 3 detectors -> ONE ledger; per-aspect retry cap; UNFILLED+disclosed
206	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
207	
208	# iter-2 P1-1 fix: a fresh checklist round re-words the SAME underlying gap almost every
209	# time (GLM does not reproduce its own exact phrasing across calls). Exact-string keying
210	# then spawns a brand-new PENDING todo each round and the per-aspect retry cap never bites
211	# — the loop re-fetches the same aspect forever. This is a ROUTING dedup (does this new
212	# phrase point at a todo we already have?), not a quality/faithfulness judgment about the
213	# corpus, so token-overlap collapse is in-scope here even though the SAME mechanism is
214	# banned for faithfulness verdicts (§-1.1 ghost-mindset) — those are two different jobs.
215	_ASPECT_DEDUP_JACCARD_THRESHOLD_DEFAULT = 0.4
216	_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
217	    "a", "an", "the", "and", "or", "of", "in", "on", "for", "to", "with", "vs", "versus",
218	    "is", "are", "was", "were", "be", "been", "its", "it", "this", "that", "any", "no",
219	    "not", "at", "by", "from", "as", "about", "into", "over", "than", "other",
220	})
221	
222	
223	def _canon_tokens(text: str) -> frozenset[str]:
224	    """Lowercase, punctuation-stripped, stopword-dropped token SET — the canonical form used
225	    ONLY to collapse checklist paraphrases onto one ledger todo (see module note above)."""
226	    import re as _re
227	    words = _re.findall(r"[a-z0-9]+", str(text or "").lower())
228	    return frozenset(w for w in words if w and w not in _STOPWORDS)
229	
230	
231	def _jaccard(a: frozenset, b: frozenset) -> float:
232	    if not a and not b:
233	        return 1.0
234	    if not a or not b:
235	        return 0.0
236	    inter = len(a & b)
237	    union = len(a | b)
238	    return inter / union if union else 0.0
239	
240	
241	def _normalize_section_label(section: str) -> str:
242	    """Whitespace/case-insensitive key for ``OutlineWorkspace.unhomeable_sections`` — a section
243	    LABEL (not a specific aspect wording) is what's proven unhomeable, so this key deliberately
244	    ignores aspect text entirely (see the field's docstring)."""
245	    return " ".join(str(section or "").strip().lower().split())
246	
247	
248	def _aspect_dedup_threshold() -> float:
249	    try:
250	        return float(os.getenv(
251	            "PG_OUTLINE_ASPECT_DEDUP_JACCARD", str(_ASPECT_DEDUP_JACCARD_THRESHOLD_DEFAULT),
252	        ))
253	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
254	        return _ASPECT_DEDUP_JACCARD_THRESHOLD_DEFAULT
255	
256	
257	# iter-4 P0-1 fix knobs (real-corpus proof the checklist only ever proposed a phantom
258	# "Summary Table" section under the locked 4-section DRB-72 deliverable, then correctly
259	# vetoed it, leaving zero genuine within-section gaps found): two mechanical (never-LLM)
260	# thresholds that let a checklist/agent-proposed section label that does not resolve to a
261	# current outline title EXACTLY still be recognized/routed instead of silently vetoed.
262	_SECTION_TITLE_FUZZY_JACCARD_DEFAULT = 0.5
263	_SECTION_REMAP_JACCARD_DEFAULT = 0.06
264	
265	
266	def _section_title_fuzzy_threshold() -> float:
267	    """Threshold for recognizing a proposed section label as the SAME section under a
268	    different/short wording (e.g. 'Summary Table' vs 'Summary Table: Application Cases,
269	    Impacts, and Risks by Industry/Occupation') — high bar, this is identity recognition."""
270	    return _env_float(
271	        "PG_OUTLINE_SECTION_TITLE_FUZZY_JACCARD", _SECTION_TITLE_FUZZY_JACCARD_DEFAULT,
272	    )
273	
274	
275	def _section_remap_jaccard_threshold() -> float:
276	    """Threshold for remapping a facet to the EXISTING section whose own topic (title+focus)
277	    it most overlaps, when no section in the outline is even fuzzily the SAME section — low
278	    bar by design: under a locked required-structure deliverable, a genuine content facet
279	    must live in SOME existing section, never invent a new one, and 'weight don't filter'
280	    (§-1.3) means routing beats discarding whenever there is ANY real signal."""
281	    return _env_float("PG_OUTLINE_SECTION_REMAP_JACCARD", _SECTION_REMAP_JACCARD_DEFAULT)
282	
283	
284	# iter-2 P0-1 fix: the checklist's anti-invention grounding gate. A candidate deficiency line
285	# must carry a verbatim quote from the research question; these two helpers are the mechanical
286	# (never-LLM) check that the quote is real, not a paraphrase or a hallucinated snippet.
287	_MIN_GROUNDING_QUOTE_WORDS = 2
288	
289	
290	_QUOTE_WRAP_CHARS = "\"'‘’“”‹›«»`"
291	
292	
293	def _normalize_for_quote_check(text: str) -> str:
294	    import re as _re
295	    stripped = str(text or "").strip().strip(_QUOTE_WRAP_CHARS).strip()
296	    return _re.sub(r"\s+", " ", stripped.lower())
297	
298	
299	def _quote_is_grounded(quote: str, question_norm: str) -> bool:
300	    """True iff ``quote`` (whitespace/case-normalized, and with any wrapping quote-mark
301	    PUNCTUATION the model added around its own answer stripped — iter-4 robustness fix: a
302	    model reply like the literal three characters ``"foo bar"`` around an otherwise-correct
303	    verbatim excerpt must not be mechanically rejected just because those wrapping marks are
304	    not themselves part of the source text) is a literal substring of the (already-normalized)
305	    research question AND carries at least ``_MIN_GROUNDING_QUOTE_WORDS`` words (a bare
306	    one-word quote like the subject's name is not a specific-enough justification for a
307	    claimed missing FACET)."""
308	    q = _normalize_for_quote_check(quote)
309	    if not q or len(q.split()) < _MIN_GROUNDING_QUOTE_WORDS:
310	        return False
311	    return q in question_norm
312

314	@dataclass
315	class GapTodo:
316	    section: str
317	    aspect: str
318	    needed_kind: str = "coverage"   # "coverage" | "numeric_rows" | "density"
319	    status: str = "PENDING"          # PENDING | IN_PROGRESS | COMPLETE | UNFILLED
320	    attempts: int = 0
321	    source: str = "checklist"        # checklist | tool_failure | agent
322	    disclosure: str = ""
323	
324	    def key(self) -> tuple[str, str]:
325	        return (str(self.section), str(self.aspect))
326	
327	
328	class GapLedger:
329	    """Dedup-by-(section,aspect) EXACT match, falling back to same-section token-overlap
330	    (Jaccard >= threshold) paraphrase collapse; per-aspect retry cap; UNFILLED + disclosed
331	    on exhaustion.
332	
333	    §-1.3.1 / §-1.1: this ledger is a ROUTING signal (what to search next), never a quality
334	    verdict about the corpus — it names todos, it does not score the pipeline's own output.
335	    """
336	
337	    def __init__(self, max_retries_per_aspect: Optional[int] = None):
338	        self._todos: dict[tuple[str, str], GapTodo] = {}
339	        self._max_retries = (
340	            max_retries_per_aspect
341	            if max_retries_per_aspect is not None
342	            else _gap_retries_per_aspect()
343	        )
344	
345	    def _find_paraphrase(self, section: str, aspect: str) -> Optional[GapTodo]:
346	        """Same-section, token-overlap match against an EXISTING todo (any status). Iter-2
347	        P1-1: collapses re-worded checklist gaps onto the one todo already tracking that
348	        aspect so the per-aspect retry cap actually bites."""
349	        canon_new = _canon_tokens(aspect)
350	        if not canon_new:
351	            return None
352	        threshold = _aspect_dedup_threshold()
353	        best: Optional[GapTodo] = None
354	        best_score = 0.0
355	        for (sec2, asp2), todo in self._todos.items():
356	            if sec2 != section:
357	                continue
358	            score = _jaccard(canon_new, _canon_tokens(asp2))
359	            if score >= threshold and score > best_score:
360	                best, best_score = todo, score
361	        return best
362	
363	    def add(
364	        self, section: str, aspect: str,
365	        needed_kind: str = "coverage", source: str = "checklist",
366	    ) -> GapTodo:
367	        section = str(section or "").strip()
368	        aspect = str(aspect or "").strip()
369	        key = (section, aspect)
370	        existing = self._todos.get(key)
371	        if existing is not None:
372	            return existing
373	        paraphrase = self._find_paraphrase(section, aspect)
374	        if paraphrase is not None:
375	            return paraphrase
376	        todo = GapTodo(
377	            section=section, aspect=aspect, needed_kind=needed_kind, source=source,
378	        )
379	        self._todos[key] = todo
380	        return todo
381	
382	    def add_unfillable(
383	        self, section: str, aspect: str, reason: str,
384	        needed_kind: str = "coverage", source: str = "checklist",
385	    ) -> GapTodo:
386	        """Iter-3 P0 fix (real-corpus degeneration — 'Summary Table' gap re-fired ~10x over
387	        945s while every fetched row stayed orphaned in the pool): a gap whose named section
388	        can NEVER be routed to retrieval — it does not match any current outline title, and
389	        under a locked required-structure deliverable there is no way to add a new section for
390	        it — is a STRUCTURAL limitation, not a content gap. Recording it UNFILLED immediately
391	        (never PENDING) means ``next_pending()`` never selects it and the decide-loop never
392	        fires ``search_more_evidence`` for it, so the loop cannot burn its turn/wall budget
393	        re-fetching the same unhomeable aspect under a slightly different checklist wording
394	        each round. Still disclosed (§-1.3 — no silent drop), just never retried."""
395	        todo = self.add(section=section, aspect=aspect, needed_kind=needed_kind, source=source)
396	        if todo.status not in ("UNFILLED", "COMPLETE"):
397	            todo.status = "UNFILLED"
398	            todo.disclosure = reason
399	        return todo
400	
401	    def get(self, section: str, aspect: str) -> Optional[GapTodo]:
402	        return self._todos.get((str(section or "").strip(), str(aspect or "").strip()))
403	
404	    def next_pending(self) -> Optional[GapTodo]:
405	        for todo in self._todos.values():
406	            if todo.status == "PENDING":
407	                return todo
408	        return None
409	
410	    def mark_in_progress(self, todo: GapTodo) -> None:
411	        todo.status = "IN_PROGRESS"
412	        todo.attempts += 1
413	
414	    def mark_complete(self, todo: GapTodo) -> None:
415	        todo.status = "COMPLETE"
416	
417	    def mark_retry_or_unfilled(self, todo: GapTodo, reason: str) -> None:
418	        """Per-aspect retry: PENDING again while attempts < cap; UNFILLED + disclosed at cap."""
419	        if todo.attempts >= self._max_retries:
420	            todo.status = "UNFILLED"
421	            todo.disclosure = reason
422	        else:
423	            todo.status = "PENDING"
424	
425	    @property
426	    def pending_count(self) -> int:
427	        return sum(1 for t in self._todos.values() if t.status == "PENDING")
428	
429	    @property
430	    def unfilled(self) -> list[GapTodo]:
431	        return [t for t in self._todos.values() if t.status == "UNFILLED"]
432	
433	    @property
434	    def all_todos(self) -> list[GapTodo]:
435	        return list(self._todos.values())
436	
437	    def as_list(self) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
438	        return [dataclasses.asdict(t) for t in self._todos.values()]
439	
440	    def summary_for_llm(self) -> str:
441	        if not self._todos:
442	            return "  (empty — no gaps identified yet)"
443	        lines = []
444	        for t in self._todos.values():
445	            lines.append(
446	                f"  [{t.status}] section={t.section!r} aspect={t.aspect!r} "
447	                f"kind={t.needed_kind} attempts={t.attempts}/{self._max_retries} source={t.source}"
448	            )
449	        return "\n".join(lines)
450	
451	
452	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
453	# OutlineWorkspace — the agent's mutable state (checkpointed after every mutation)
454	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
455	
456	@dataclass
457	class OutlineWorkspace:
458	    research_question: str
459	    ev_store: dict[str, dict[str, Any]]              # cp2 rows keyed by evidence_id, S2-stamped
460	    outline_draft: list[Any] = field(default_factory=list)   # list[SectionPlan]
461	    gap_ledger: GapLedger = field(default_factory=GapLedger)
462	    notebook: Optional[AnalysisNotebook] = None
463	    basket_menu: Any = None                            # outline_digest menu (or None)
464	    budgets: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
465	    disclosures: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
466	    # W4: successful execute_python payloads, so a computed value is no longer discarded at
467	    # every exit. EXPLORATORY / NOT RENDERABLE — every row carries renderable=False +
468	    # bar_reason (see _compute_result_check). Telemetry + planner provenance only; this is
469	    # NOT a render path and never reaches a writer prompt.
470	    compute_results: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
471	    # W3-render (2026-07-11): run-scoped registry of VERIFIED quantified models, keyed by
472	    # (model_id, spec_hash) exactly as ``strict_verify(quantified_models=...)`` expects. This is
473	    # the ONLY compute-render surface: a number lands here solely by re-derivation through the
474	    # fail-closed ModelSpec lane (verified_compute.run_verified_compute -> build_quantified_spec ->
475	    # execute_quantified_model), and renders solely via its ``[#calc:model:hash:field]`` token,
476	    # which strict_verify force-routes to ``verify_modeled_atom``. Exploratory execute_python output
477	    # (compute_results above, renderable=False) NEVER enters this dict — that separation is the
478	    # faithfulness invariant (a derived number can never reach the [#ev]/[CITE] render path).
479	    quantified_models: dict = field(default_factory=dict)  # (model_id, spec_hash) -> QuantifiedResult
480	    # MOAT DETERMINISTIC EMISSION (2026-07-11): the render-ready [#calc:] claim SENTENCES the agent
481	    # actually derived through ``verified_compute``, so the FULL-CORPUS composer can inject them
482	    # into the target section body deterministically (an LLM writer cannot be trusted to copy an
483	    # unguessable spec_hash verbatim). Each record:
484	    #   {"section": <target section title or "" for auto-home>, "sentence": <render_sentence>,
485	    #    "calc_token": <[#calc:model:hash:field]>, "input_ev_ids": [<ev_id>, ...]}
486	    # Populated by the ``verified_compute`` tool alongside the ``quantified_models`` registration.
487	    # The sentence is NON-RENDERING on its own: it only survives strict_verify if that same
488	    # registry verifies its token (verify_modeled_atom), so emission stays fail-closed and a
489	    # derived number STILL cannot reach the [#ev:]/[CITE:] path.
490	    computed_claims: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
491	    turn: int = 0
492	    started_monotonic: float = field(default_factory=time.monotonic)
493	    checkpoint_dir: Optional[str] = None
494	    total_input_tokens: int = 0
495	    total_output_tokens: int = 0
496	    # Iter-2 fix: the deliverable's REQUIRED section titles (empty => no structure lock, the
497	    # common case for the free-form acceptance/real-corpus runs). When non-empty, the outline's
498	    # section STRUCTURE is governed by the caller (S4 ORCH-2 PUSH 1 / `_call_outline`'s own
499	    # `required_sections` conform already enforced this at SEED time) and this module must never
500	    # silently add a 5th section behind the caller's back — see `_tool_update_outline` and the
501	    # auto-assign fallback in `OutlineAgent._execute_tool`.
502	    required_titles: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
503	    # RACE-FLOOR fix (2026-07-12, this wheel): the corpus-DERIVED thematic-coverage floor. The SEED
504	    # outline (built by ``_call_outline`` from the evidence/baskets — query-agnostic, NOT a hardcoded
505	    # task-72 structure) is the corpus's own theme decomposition. Two byte-identical renders diverged
506	    # (RACE 0.4447 vs 0.3518) because the agentic loop, over more turns, freely issued ``merge`` ops
507	    # that COLLAPSED distinct corpus themes (e.g. Wage-Inequality, Policy) into fewer, thinner
508	    # sections — pure non-determinism that thinned comprehensiveness. This floor caps NET thematic
509	    # reduction: the loop may still split / add / retitle / reassign / search (all coverage-improving),
510	    # but a ``merge`` that would drop the live section count below the seed count is DEFERRED as a
511	    # disclosed no-op. GENERAL (floor = seed's own section count), faithfulness-NEUTRAL (pure
512	    # structural placement; strict_verify / NLI / [#calc] lane untouched). 0 => no floor (legacy).
513	    min_sections: int = 0
514	    # Iter-3 P0 fix (real-corpus THINNED run: 8 search_more_evidence calls, 31 genuinely NEW
515	    # fetched rows, ZERO landed in any required section — the whole retrieval budget was spent
516	    # re-chasing the SAME unhomeable section label, e.g. "Summary Table", under 8 differently
517	    # worded aspects that each failed the (section,aspect) paraphrase dedup because the ASPECT
518	    # text varied enough while the SECTION stayed constant). Once a section label is proven
519	    # unhomeable (checklist- or agent-initiated), it is banked here (normalized) and vetoed —
520	    # cheaply, before any real fetch — for the rest of the run, regardless of aspect rewording.
521	    unhomeable_sections: set = field(default_factory=set)
522	    # S3 FEED (Research Planning Gate ↔ outline agent seam). All default to the
523	    # inert/empty value so a workspace built without a gate artifact (every
524	    # champion caller today) is BYTE-IDENTICAL: no scope threaded into the gap
525	    # search, no term-ledger validation on update_outline, no seeded gaps.
526	    #
527	    # ``gate_research_frame`` / ``gate_gap_protocol``: THE BUG FIX. The gate's
528	    # retrieval scope (the SAME ``research_frame`` + anchor ``protocol`` used by
529	    # initial retrieval), threaded into ``_tool_search_more_evidence``'s
530	    # ``run_live_retrieval`` call so the outline-stage gap search stays IN SCOPE
531	    # instead of running unscoped (both designers found this bug). None => the
532	    # champion ``run_live_retrieval`` defaults (research_frame=None/protocol=None).
533	    gate_research_frame: Any = None
534	    gate_gap_protocol: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None
535	    # ``contract_hash``: the pinned artifact's contract SHA (provenance only — the
536	    # resolved outline records which gate seed it refined). "" => no gate.
537	    contract_hash: str = ""
538	    # ``term_ledger``: {binding_term_owners, locked_titles, ordered_titles,
539	    # binding_term_ids}. When non-empty, ``_tool_update_outline`` rejects a
540	    # revision that drops an explicit title lock or the LAST owner of a binding
541	    # term (build via ``outline_gate_feed.build_term_ledger``). Empty => no ledger
542	    # validation (champion behavior).
543	    term_ledger: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
544	    _checkpoint_seq: int = 0
545	
546	    def next_ev_offset(self) -> int:
547	        """Highest numeric suffix across current ev_ids, +1. Rows that don't parse as
548	        ``ev_<digits>`` are ignored for the offset computation (never crash the offset)."""
549	        mx = -1
550	        for eid in self.ev_store.keys():
551	            tail = str(eid).rsplit("_", 1)[-1]
552	            if tail.isdigit():
553	                mx = max(mx, int(tail))
554	        return mx + 1
555	
556	    def existing_urls(self) -> set[str]:
557	        urls: set[str] = set()
558	        for row in self.ev_store.values():
559	            if isinstance(row, dict):
560	                u = str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "").strip()
561	                if u:
562	                    urls.add(u)
563	        return urls
564	
565	    def disclose(self, text: str) -> None:
566	        self.disclosures.append(text)
567	        logger.info("[outline_agent] %s", text)
568	
569	    def elapsed_seconds(self) -> float:
570	        return time.monotonic() - self.started_monotonic
571	
572	    def checkpoint(self, event: str) -> None:
573	        """Best-effort checkpoint-after-every-mutation (crash-resume). NEVER raises — a
574	        checkpoint failure must not abort the loop (mirrors ``write_cp4_outline_snapshot``'s
575	        own best-effort contract one layer up)."""
576	        if not self.checkpoint_dir:
577	            return
578	        try:
579	            from src.polaris_graph.generator.outline_checkpoint import (  # noqa: PLC0415
580	                build_cp4_payload, write_cp4_outline_snapshot,
581	            )
582	            self._checkpoint_seq += 1
583	            plans_dicts = [_plan_to_dict(p) for p in self.outline_draft]
584	            payload = build_cp4_payload(
585	                question_sha="",
586	                upstream=None,
587	                run_config_sha="",
588	                flag_slate={"PG_OUTLINE_AGENT": "1", "event": str(event)},
589	                adjustments_applied=None,
590	                final_plans=plans_dicts,
591	                revision_audit={
592	                    "gap_ledger": self.gap_ledger.as_list(),
593	                    "disclosures": list(self.disclosures),
594	                    "turn": self.turn,
595	                    "checkpoint_seq": self._checkpoint_seq,
596	                    "notebook_steps": (
597	                        [
598	                            {
599	                                "tool_name": s.tool_name,
600	                                "reasoning": s.reasoning[:200],
601	                                "success": s.result.success,
602	                            }
603	                            for s in self.notebook.steps
604	                        ] if self.notebook is not None else []
605	                    ),
606	                },
607	                digest_stats={
608	                    "ev_store_size": len(self.ev_store),
609	                    "elapsed_seconds": round(self.elapsed_seconds(), 1),
610	                },
611	            )
612	            write_cp4_outline_snapshot(self.checkpoint_dir, payload)
613	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — checkpoint is best-effort, never fatal
614	            logger.warning("[outline_agent] checkpoint write skipped (fail-open): %s", exc)
615	
616	
617	def _plan_field(plan: Any, key: str, default: Any = "") -> Any:
618	    """Iter-2 fix (found via offline smoke test, not in the original Fable P0/P1 list): plans in
619	    ``workspace.outline_draft`` are NOT a uniform type across the loop's lifetime — the SEED plans
620	    from ``_call_outline`` are ``SectionPlan`` dataclass instances, but ``outline_revise.apply_
621	    revision_ops`` (which BOTH ``update_outline`` and the new iter-2 auto-assign call after every
622	    successful search) always returns plain ``dict`` plans (``RevisionApplyResult.new_plans:
623	    list[dict]``). A bare ``getattr(p, 'title', default)`` silently returns ``default`` for a dict
624	    (dicts have no attribute access) — so the FIRST time any update_outline/auto-assign op ran,
625	    every downstream title lookup in THIS module (decide's outline summary, the checklist's
626	    section block, ``inspect_basket``'s assignment check, auto-assign's own section-title
627	    resolver) would have silently gone blank, breaking the very mutation this iteration exists to
628	    fix. Mirrors ``outline_revise._plan_get`` exactly (dict-or-attribute duck typing) — reproduced
629	    here rather than imported to keep this module's OFF-path import graph unchanged (LAW VI/V:
630	    small, side-effect-free helper; not worth a cross-module coupling for 4 lines)."""
631	    if isinstance(plan, dict):
632	        return plan.get(key, default)
633	    return getattr(plan, key, default)
634	
635	
636	def _plan_to_dict(plan: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
637	    if isinstance(plan, dict):
638	        return dict(plan)
639	    if dataclasses.is_dataclass(plan):
640	        return dataclasses.asdict(plan)
641	    return {
642	        "title": _plan_field(plan, "title", ""),
643	        "focus": _plan_field(plan, "focus", ""),
644	        "ev_ids": list(_plan_field(plan, "ev_ids", []) or []),
645	        "basket_ids": list(_plan_field(plan, "basket_ids", []) or []),
646	        "archetype": _plan_field(plan, "archetype", ""),
647	        "undersupplied": _plan_field(plan, "undersupplied", False),
648	    }
649	
650	
651	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
652	# search_more_evidence — the retrieval pipe as a tool (the sharp seam)
653	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
654	
655	def _sync_llm_bridge(model: str, max_tokens: int) -> Callable[[str], str]:
656	    """Build a SYNCHRONOUS ``str -> str`` callable bridging into the async
657	    ``OpenRouterClient.generate`` — the exact thread+contextvars pattern
658	    ``run_honest_sweep_r3.py``'s ``_topic_llm`` uses (that module is not importable here without
659	    dragging in the whole sweep script, so the ~20-line bridge is reproduced, not re-derived).
660	    Required because ``classify_topic_relevance`` (topic_relevance_gate.py) takes a sync
661	    callable and this module runs inside an ALREADY-RUNNING asyncio loop (the outline agent's
662	    own loop), where a bare ``asyncio.run()`` would raise."""
663	
664	    def _llm(prompt: str) -> str:
665	        import concurrent.futures as _futures
666	        import contextvars as _contextvars
667	
668	        async def _run() -> str:
669	            from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415
670	            client = OpenRouterClient(model=model)
671	            try:
672	                resp = await client.generate(prompt=prompt, max_tokens=max_tokens, temperature=0.0)
673	                return (resp.content or "").strip()
674	            finally:
675	                if hasattr(client, "close"):
676	                    try:
677	                        await client.close()
678	                    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — client close is best-effort
679	                        pass
680	
681	        parent_ctx = _contextvars.copy_context()
682	
683	        def _worker() -> str:
684	            return parent_ctx.run(lambda: asyncio.run(_run()))
685	
686	        with _futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) as pool:
687	            return pool.submit(_worker).result()
688	
689	    return _llm
690	
691	
692	async def _tool_search_more_evidence(
693	    workspace: OutlineWorkspace,
694	    agent_model: str,
695	    *,
696	    section: str = "",
697	    aspect: str = "",
698	    domain: Optional[str] = None,
699	    retrieval_deadline_monotonic: Optional[float] = None,
700	    **_ignored: Any,
701	) -> ToolResult:
702	    """The retrieval pipe as a ReAct tool. Steps (design §): per-todo query derivation
703	    (scope-anchored, ``_is_status_leak`` guard) -> ``run_live_retrieval(anchor_seed=False)`` ->
704	    ``merge_retrieval_results`` -> offset-renumber (hard assert) + URL-dedup vs existing ->
705	    S2 stamp pass (chrome delete + topic-judge fail-open) -> fold into ``ev_store`` -> disclosed
706	    ``ToolResult``. One retrieval in flight at a time (§8.4 — this coroutine is awaited, never
707	    fired concurrently by the driver)."""
708	    aspect_text = str(aspect or "").strip() or str(section or "").strip()
709	    if not aspect_text:
710	        return ToolResult(
711	            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
712	            markdown="search_more_evidence requires a `section` or `aspect`.",
713	            error="missing_aspect",
714	        )
715	
716	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.fs_researcher_query_gen import (  # noqa: PLC0415
717	        _is_status_leak,
718	    )
719	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415
720	
721	    query_derive_max_tokens = _env_int(
722	        "PG_OUTLINE_QUERY_DERIVE_MAX_TOKENS", PG_OUTLINE_QUERY_DERIVE_MAX_TOKENS_DEFAULT,
723	    )
724	    client = OpenRouterClient(model=agent_model)
725	    try:
726	        derive_prompt = (
727	            "Write ONE web-search query for the SUB-TOPIC below, kept STRICTLY within the "
728	            "scope of the RESEARCH QUESTION (carry its subject, domain and key entities; do "
729	            "NOT broaden the query into the sub-topic's generic field). Query only, one line, "
730	            "no quotes.\n\n"
731	            f"RESEARCH QUESTION:\n{workspace.research_question}\n\nSUB-TOPIC:\n{aspect_text}"
732	        )
733	        try:
734	            # Iter-6 P0 fix: same reasoning-pool bound as the checklist/decide calls above —
735	            # this call already fails open on ANY exception (including a truncation), but an
736	            # unbounded-effort reasoning prelude on a dense corpus still needlessly degrades a
737	            # one-line query derivation to the raw aspect text every time; bounding it means the
738	            # derived (scope-anchored) query actually gets used instead of the coarser fallback.
739	            resp = await client.generate(
740	                prompt=derive_prompt, max_tokens=query_derive_max_tokens, temperature=0.2,
741	                reasoning_max_tokens=_reasoning_max_tokens(),
742	            )
743	            derived = (resp.content or "").strip().splitlines()[0].strip().strip('"') if (
744	                resp.content or ""
745	            ).strip() else ""
746	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — query derivation is a small text call; degrade to raw aspect
747	            logger.warning("[outline_agent] query derivation failed, using raw aspect: %s", exc)
748	            derived = ""
749	    finally:
750	        if hasattr(client, "close"):
751	            try:
752	                await client.close()
753	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
754	                pass
755	
756	    query = derived or aspect_text
757	    if _is_status_leak(query, workspace.research_question):
758	        return ToolResult(
759	            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
760	            markdown=f"Derived query was a status-leak, aborted: {query[:120]!r}",
761	            error="status_leak_aborted",
762	        )
763	
764	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.live_retriever import (  # noqa: PLC0415
765	        LiveRetrievalResult, run_live_retrieval,
766	    )
767	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.fs_researcher_query_gen import (  # noqa: PLC0415
768	        merge_retrieval_results,
769	    )
770	
771	    t0 = time.monotonic()
772	    try:
773	        # BUG FOUND IN W1 LIVE ACCEPTANCE RUN (fixed same-iter): ``anchor_seed=False`` makes
774	        # ``run_live_retrieval`` build ``all_queries`` from ``amplified_queries`` ONLY (the
775	        # verbatim ``research_question`` is NOT auto-fired when the anchor is suppressed — see
776	        # the function's own docstring). The gap query MUST be passed via ``amplified_queries``;
777	        # ``research_question`` stays the WORKSPACE question (scope-validator anchor text) so a
778	        # scoped sub-query is validated against the real topic, not against itself.
779	        # S3 BUG FIX (both designers): thread the gate's retrieval SCOPE into the
780	        # outline-stage gap search. Before this, ``research_frame`` / ``protocol``
781	        # were silently dropped here, so a gap search run under a hard journal /
782	        # English / jurisdiction contract went out UNSCOPED and could dilute a
783	        # correctly-scoped first pass. When the workspace carries no gate scope
784	        # (every champion caller — both stay None/None), this is byte-identical to
785	        # the champion ``run_live_retrieval`` defaults. A HARD-scope frame is never
786	        # a post-fetch filter here: it ROUTES the SAME backends/anchor the initial
787	        # retrieval used, so the gap query keeps that scope from its first fetch.
788	        raw_result = await asyncio.to_thread(
789	            run_live_retrieval,
790	            research_question=workspace.research_question,
791	            amplified_queries=[query],
792	            domain=domain,
793	            anchor_seed=False,
794	            retrieval_deadline_monotonic=retrieval_deadline_monotonic,
795	            research_frame=workspace.gate_research_frame,
796	            protocol=workspace.gate_gap_protocol,
797	        )
798	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a single retrieval round must not crash the loop
799	        return ToolResult(
800	            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
801	            markdown=f"run_live_retrieval raised for query {query!r}: {exc}",
802	            error=str(exc)[:500],
803	        )
804	    elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0
805	
806	    merged = merge_retrieval_results([raw_result], LiveRetrievalResult)
807	    fetched_rows = list(getattr(merged, "evidence_rows", None) or [])
808	    n_fetched_of = f"{len(fetched_rows)} of {getattr(merged, 'candidates_total', len(fetched_rows))}"
809	
810	    # THE shared fold-in seam (outline/_fold_in.py): url-dedup -> offset-renumber (hard
811	    # id-collision assert) -> S2 stamp/delete -> insert. fetch_url re-enters through the same unit.
812	    fold = await fold_in_fetched_rows(
813	        workspace, fetched_rows,
814	        research_question=workspace.research_question, agent_model=agent_model,
815	    )
816	    kept_rows = fold.kept_rows
817	    n_kept = fold.n_kept
818	    n_deleted = fold.n_deleted
819	    disclosure = (
820	        f"search_more_evidence[{aspect_text[:60]!r}] query={query!r} fetched {n_fetched_of}, "
821	        f"url-dup dropped {fold.n_url_dup}, kept {n_kept}, deleted {n_deleted} "
822	        f"(chrome={len(fold.deleted_chrome)}, off-topic={len(fold.deleted_offtopic)}) in {elapsed:.1f}s"
823	    )
824	    workspace.disclose(disclosure)
825	
826	    md_lines = [f"**{disclosure}**", ""]
827	    for row in kept_rows[:10]:
828	        md_lines.append(
829	            f"- {row.get('evidence_id')}: {str(row.get('title') or '')[:80]} "
830	            f"[CITE:{row.get('evidence_id')}]"
831	        )
832	    if len(kept_rows) > 10:
833	        md_lines.append(f"- ... and {len(kept_rows) - 10} more")
834	
835	    return ToolResult(
836	        success=n_kept > 0,
837	        tool_name="search_more_evidence",
838	        markdown="\n".join(md_lines),
839	        source_evidence_ids=[r["evidence_id"] for r in kept_rows],
840	        error=("" if n_kept > 0 else "no_new_evidence_survived_screen"),
841	    )
842	
843	
844	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
845	# inspect_basket
846	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
847	
848	def _tool_list_tools(
849	    registry: "ToolRegistry", *, name: str = "", category: str = "", **_ignored: Any,
850	) -> ToolResult:
851	    """Meta-tool: return the FULL spec (description + params) of ONE tool by ``name``, or a one-line
852	    listing of every tool in ``category``. The on-demand discovery path for the decide menu's
853	    collapsed non-core index (redesign 'Scaling the decide step past 20 tools'). No LLM, no network."""
854	    nm = str(name or "").strip()
855	    cat = str(category or "").strip().lower()
856	    if nm:
857	        tool = registry.get_tool(nm)
858	        if tool is None:
859	            return ToolResult(
860	                success=False, tool_name="list_tools",
861	                markdown=f"No tool named {nm!r}.", error="tool_not_found",
862	            )
863	        md = "\n".join(registry._describe_full(tool, True))  # noqa: SLF001 — same package intent
864	        return ToolResult(success=True, tool_name="list_tools", markdown=md)
865	    matches = [
866	        t for t in (registry.get_tool(n) for n in registry.available_tools(True))
867	        if t is not None and (not cat or cat in [str(x).lower() for x in (t.tags or [])])
868	    ]
869	    if not matches:
870	        return ToolResult(
871	            success=False, tool_name="list_tools",
872	            markdown=f"No tools in category {cat!r}." if cat else "No tools registered.",
873	            error="no_tools_in_category",
874	        )
875	    lines = [f"**{len(matches)} tool(s)"
876	             f"{f' in [{cat}]' if cat else ''}:**"]
877	    for t in sorted(matches, key=lambda x: x.name):
878	        lines.append(f"- {t.name}: {t.description.split('. ')[0][:100]}")
879	    return ToolResult(success=True, tool_name="list_tools", markdown="\n".join(lines))
880	
881	
882	async def _tool_inspect_basket(
883	    workspace: OutlineWorkspace, *, basket_id: str = "", **_ignored: Any,
884	) -> ToolResult:
885	    """Pure read of the cp3 basket digest — members, corroboration, whether every member is
886	    already assigned to a section. No LLM, no network."""
887	    menu = workspace.basket_menu
888	    if menu is None or not basket_id:
889	        return ToolResult(
890	            success=False, tool_name="inspect_basket",
891	            markdown="No basket digest available or no basket_id supplied.",
892	            error="no_digest_or_id",
893	        )
894	    members = list(getattr(menu, "basket_member_ev_ids", {}).get(basket_id, []) or [])
895	    corroboration = getattr(menu, "basket_work_corroboration", {}).get(basket_id, 0)
896	    if not members:
897	        return ToolResult(
898	            success=False, tool_name="inspect_basket",
899	            markdown=f"Basket {basket_id!r} not found in the digest.",
900	            error="basket_not_found",
901	        )
902	    assigned_ev = {eid for p in workspace.outline_draft for eid in (_plan_field(p, "ev_ids", None) or [])}
903	    unassigned = [m for m in members if m not in assigned_ev]
904	    lines = [
905	        f"**Basket {basket_id}**: {len(members)} member(s), corroboration={corroboration}, "
906	        f"unassigned={len(unassigned)}",
907	    ]
908	    for m in members[:15]:
909	        row = workspace.ev_store.get(m, {})
910	        lines.append(f"- {m}: {str(row.get('title') or '')[:70]} [CITE:{m}]")
911	    return ToolResult(
912	        success=True, tool_name="inspect_basket", markdown="\n".join(lines),
913	        source_evidence_ids=members,
914	    )
915	
916	
917	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
918	# update_outline — wraps outline_revise parse/apply (validated, per-op reject, never free rewrite)
919	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
920	
921	async def _tool_update_outline(
922	    workspace: OutlineWorkspace, *, ops: Any = None, **_ignored: Any,
923	) -> ToolResult:
924	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.outline_revise import (  # noqa: PLC0415
925	        apply_revision_ops, parse_revision_ops,
926	    )
927	
928	    if ops is None:
929	        return ToolResult(
930	            success=False, tool_name="update_outline",
931	            markdown="update_outline requires `ops` (a revision op list).",
932	            error="missing_ops",
933	        )
934	    allowed_ev_ids = set(workspace.ev_store.keys())
935	    plan_titles = [str(_plan_field(p, "title", "")) for p in workspace.outline_draft]
936	    raw = ops if isinstance(ops, (dict, str)) else {"ops": ops}
937	    parse_result = parse_revision_ops(
938	        raw, allowed_ev_ids=allowed_ev_ids, plan_titles=plan_titles,
939	    )
940	    if parse_result.parse_failed or not parse_result.ops:
941	        return ToolResult(
942	            success=False, tool_name="update_outline",
943	            markdown=(
944	                f"No ops applied (parse_failed={parse_result.parse_failed}, "
945	                f"rejected={parse_result.rejected})."
946	            ),
947	            error="no_ops_applied",
948	        )
949	
950	    # Iter-2 fix: thread the deliverable's required-structure lock (if any) into apply — mirrors
951	    # `scripts/orchestrator_lab/outline_lab.py`'s `_required` threading. Without this, a
952	    # required_sections-governed outline (e.g. the DRB-72 4-section canonical structure) could
953	    # silently grow a 5th section via an agent-issued `add` op, breaking the caller's exact-N-in-
954	    # order structural contract that `_call_outline` already conformed the seed to.
955	    # Snapshot the pre-op section titles so the S3 term-ledger guard can compare
956	    # them against the post-op set (below). Cheap; only used when a ledger exists.
957	    _titles_before = [str(_plan_field(p, "title", "")) for p in workspace.outline_draft]
958	
959	    apply_result = apply_revision_ops(
960	        workspace.outline_draft, parse_result,
961	        required_titles=(workspace.required_titles or None),
962	        min_sections=(workspace.min_sections or 0),
963	    )
964	
965	    # ── S3 term-ledger guard: reject a revision that drops an EXPLICIT title lock
966	    # or the LAST owner of a binding term (design §6). This is a SECOND, coverage-
967	    # aware guard on top of ``apply_revision_ops``'s ``required_titles`` structural
968	    # lock: the required-titles lock protects an exact-N-in-order structure, while
969	    # the term ledger protects an explicit heading / a binding requirement's last
970	    # home even when no full structure lock is present. When the workspace carries
971	    # no ledger (every champion caller — ``term_ledger`` empty) this is a no-op and
972	    # byte-identical. On a violation the WHOLE op set is refused (the seed stands),
973	    # disclosed, never silently applied — the loop may retry a compliant revision.
974	    if workspace.term_ledger:
975	        try:
976	            from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_gate_feed import (  # noqa: PLC0415
977	                validate_revision_against_ledger,
978	            )
979	            _titles_after = [str(d.get("title", "")) for d in apply_result.new_plans]
980	            _violations = validate_revision_against_ledger(
981	                workspace.term_ledger,
982	                titles_before=_titles_before,
983	                titles_after=_titles_after,
984	            )
985	        except Exception as _led_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — guard is additive, never fatal
986	            _violations = []
987	            logger.warning(
988	                "[outline_agent] S3 ledger guard skipped (fail-open): %s", _led_exc,
989	            )
990	        if _violations:
991	            workspace.disclose(
992	                "update_outline REFUSED: revision would violate the pinned contract "
993	                f"term ledger ({'; '.join(_violations)}) — keeping the prior outline"
994	            )
995	            return ToolResult(
996	                success=False, tool_name="update_outline",
997	                markdown=(
998	                    "No ops applied — the revision would drop an explicit title lock "
999	                    "or the last owner of a binding contract term: "
1000	                    + "; ".join(_violations)
1001	                ),
1002	                error="contract_term_ledger_violation",
1003	            )
1004	    # Iter-2 fix (found via offline smoke test): ``apply_revision_ops`` ALWAYS returns
1005	    # ``list[dict]`` (``outline_revise`` is dict-native; it is not currently wired into
1006	    # production composition at all — grep confirms its only callers before this module were
1007	    # the offline ``outline_lab.py`` harness). But THIS module's ``workspace.outline_draft`` is
1008	    # handed straight back to ``generate_multi_section_report`` as ``parse_result.plans``, and
1009	    # every downstream composition site (M-44 primary injection, section-budget code, the
1010	    # manifest's ``[p.title for p in multi.outline]``, etc.) does direct ``.title``/``.ev_ids``
1011	    # ATTRIBUTE access on real ``SectionPlan`` dataclass instances. Handing dicts downstream
1012	    # would ``AttributeError`` the first real composition run the moment any update_outline (or
1013	    # the iter-2 auto-assign) op fires. Reconcile back to ``SectionPlan`` HERE — the one seam
1014	    # where outline_revise's dict world meets the rest of the pipeline's dataclass world — so
1015	    # every OTHER call site in this module (and every downstream consumer) can keep doing plain
1016	    # ``SectionPlan`` attribute access unconditionally.
1017	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import SectionPlan  # noqa: PLC0415
1018	    workspace.outline_draft = [
1019	        SectionPlan(
1020	            title=str(d.get("title", "")),
1021	            focus=str(d.get("focus", "")),
1022	            ev_ids=list(d.get("ev_ids", []) or []),
1023	            archetype=str(d.get("archetype", "")),
1024	            undersupplied=bool(d.get("undersupplied", False)),
1025	            basket_ids=list(d.get("basket_ids", []) or []),
1026	        )

"""S4 OUTLINE — Agentic outliner loop (W0 + W1).

Authoritative design: ``docs/fsr_build_plan.md`` section "AGENTIC OUTLINER LOOP — FABLE
AUTHORITATIVE DESIGN" (2026-07-11, effort max). This module is the THIN driver
(``OutlineAgent``, ~ design target 300 LOC for the core loop; this file additionally carries
the workspace/ledger/tool wiring the design assigns to the same file) that WIRES the existing
ReAct contract — ``ToolRegistry`` / ``ToolDefinition`` / ``ToolResult`` / ``AnalysisNotebook``
(``src/polaris_graph/tools/tool_registry.py`` + ``analysis_notebook.py``) and the
``ReactDecision`` schema (``src/polaris_graph/tools/react_agent.py``) — into a NEW small
per-turn decide/execute loop for the OUTLINE stage. It does **not** subclass the 8k-line
``ReactAnalysisAgent`` (react-mode is ~150 LOC of that file; the rest is the compose-writer,
an unrelated concern). It does **not** import smolagents.

CONTROL FLOW (verbatim from the design):
  seed via existing ``_call_outline`` (un-starved) -> sufficiency review#0 -> gap_ledger.
  Loop while turns < PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_MAX_TURNS(24): ``_decide`` picks ONE of
  {analysis tool | inspect_basket | search_more_evidence | update_outline | finish_outline};
  execute; if search: fold-in + re-check aspect; if update_outline: validated parse/apply via
  ``outline_revise``; if finish: sufficiency checklist — deficiencies+budget => bounce & continue;
  clean/exhausted => exit. EXIT: write cp4 (typed plans + unfilled_gaps[] + notebook ref + flag
  slate) via ``outline_checkpoint.build_cp4_payload`` / ``write_cp4_outline_snapshot``.

GAP TRIGGER — 3 detectors feeding ONE ``GapLedger``:
  (1) checklist (LLM, per-section exhaustive-coverage / density / compute-need) at seed,
      after every fold-in, and at every ``finish_outline`` attempt;
  (2) TOOL-FAILURE deterministic (empty comparison / zero datapoints / empty SQL / <2 studies);
  (3) agent-initiated (the decide step names a NEW aspect when calling ``search_more_evidence``).
  Per-aspect retry ``PG_OUTLINE_GAP_RETRIES_PER_ASPECT`` (default 2), then UNFILLED + disclosed;
  dedup by ``(section, aspect)``.

FAITHFULNESS: the strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance engine is UNTOUCHED and stays the
ONLY hard gate downstream of this module. Every row this module folds into the corpus is a real
fetched-and-classified row from ``run_live_retrieval`` (same production path the rest of the
pipeline uses) — this module manufactures ZERO evidence text and never marks anything "verified"
itself. The id-collision seam at fold-in is a HARD fail-loud assert (new-ids intersect
existing-ids must be empty) — see ``_offset_renumber``.

Seat: ``PG_OUTLINE_AGENT`` (default OFF). OFF => the seam at
``multi_section_generator._call_outline`` call site is a pure pass-through — byte-identical to
pre-existing behavior (see ``run_outline_agent_or_legacy`` at the bottom of this file, which is
what the generator actually calls).
"""

def _env_flag(name: str, default_on: bool) -> bool:
    raw = os.getenv(name, "1" if default_on else "0")
    return str(raw).strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES


def _env_int(name: str, default: int) -> int:
    try:
        return int(os.getenv(name, str(default)))
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return default


def _env_float(name: str, default: float) -> float:
    try:
        return float(os.getenv(name, str(default)))
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return default

# W2 P0 fix: outer belt-and-suspenders wall on the whole ``agent.run()`` call (grace

PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_RUN_TIMEOUT_GRACE_SECONDS_DEFAULT = 600
